description: Popular garden fruit grown as a warm-season annual. Available in determinate and indeterminate varieties.
harvest_instructions: Pick when fully colored and slightly soft to touch.
storage_instructions: Store at room temperature. Do not refrigerate unripe tomatoes.
growing_tips: Bury stems deep when transplanting for stronger roots
Remove suckers for indeterminate varieties
Mulch heavily to retain moisture

description: Sweet pepper available in green, red, yellow, and orange varieties.
harvest_instructions: Pick when firm and fully colored.
storage_instructions: Refrigerate in plastic bag for up to 2 weeks.
growing_tips: Start seeds early indoors as peppers need a long season
Stake plants when fruit develops

description: Spicy peppers ranging from mild jalapeno to fiery habanero.
growing_tips: Stress slightly by reducing water to increase heat
Wear gloves when harvesting hot varieties

description: Refreshing vine crop available in slicing and pickling varieties.
harvest_instructions: Pick when firm and medium-sized. Don't let them turn yellow.
storage_instructions: Refrigerate unwashed for up to 1 week.
growing_tips: Provide trellis for better fruit and space efficiency
Keep soil consistently moist
Harvest frequently to encourage production

description: Prolific summer squash that produces abundantly.
harvest_instructions: Pick when 6-8 inches long for best flavor.
growing_tips: Harvest regularly to keep plants producing
Watch for squash vine borers

description: Fast-growing squash including patty pan and crookneck varieties.
growing_tips: Pick young for tender flesh

description: Hard-shelled squash including butternut, acorn, and spaghetti varieties.
harvest_instructions: Harvest when rind is hard and stem is dry.
storage_instructions: Cure in warm area 2 weeks, then store in cool dry place for months.
growing_tips: Allow plenty of space for vines to spread
Cure before long-term storage

description: Large-fruited vine used for decoration and cooking.
growing_tips: Plant in hills of 2-3 seeds
Place cardboard under fruit to prevent rot

description: Cool-season salad green available in leaf, romaine, and head varieties.
harvest_instructions: Cut outer leaves or harvest whole head when firm.
storage_instructions: Wash, dry, and refrigerate in produce bag.
growing_tips: Succession plant every 2 weeks
Provide afternoon shade in hot weather
Bolt-resistant varieties extend harvest

description: Nutrient-dense cool-season green rich in iron and vitamins.
growing_tips: Plant in early spring or fall for best results
Bolts quickly in hot weather

description: Hardy leafy green that becomes sweeter after frost.
growing_tips: Harvest lower leaves first
Flavor improves after frost

description: Colorful leafy green with edible stalks in rainbow colors.
growing_tips: Cut-and-come-again harvesting extends production

description: Root vegetable available in many colors and sizes.
harvest_instructions: Pull when tops are about 3/4 inch diameter.
storage_instructions: Remove tops and store in sand or refrigerate.
growing_tips: Keep soil loose and free of rocks
Thin seedlings to proper spacing
Keep soil consistently moist for germination

description: Versatile root vegetable with edible leaves and roots.
growing_tips: Soak seeds overnight before planting
Each seed cluster produces multiple plants—thin early

description: Fast-growing root vegetable perfect for succession planting.
growing_tips: Harvest promptly to avoid pithy roots
Great row marker for slow-germinating crops

description: Essential kitchen staple available as short-day or long-day varieties.
harvest_instructions: Harvest when tops fall over and begin to yellow.
storage_instructions: Cure in dry, warm area for 2 weeks then store in cool, dry place.
growing_tips: Choose correct day-length variety for your latitude
Sets are easier than seed for beginners

description: Aromatic bulb planted in fall for summer harvest.
harvest_instructions: Harvest when lower leaves brown but upper leaves are still green.
storage_instructions: Cure for 2-4 weeks, then store in cool, dry place.
growing_tips: Plant cloves in fall, 4-6 weeks before ground freezes
Cut scapes in spring to redirect energy to bulb

description: Starchy tuber crop grown from seed potatoes.
harvest_instructions: Harvest new potatoes 2-3 weeks after flowering. Harvest main crop after vines die.
storage_instructions: Cure in dark place 1-2 weeks. Store in cool, dark, humid place.
growing_tips: Hill soil around stems as plants grow
Never eat green potatoes

description: Warm-season root crop grown from slips.
growing_tips: Start slips 6-8 weeks before transplanting
Cure harvested tubers at 85°F for 10 days

description: Warm-season legume available as bush or pole varieties.
harvest_instructions: Pick when pods are firm and before seeds bulge.
growing_tips: Don't soak seeds before planting
Inoculate with rhizobia for nitrogen fixing
Pole beans need sturdy support

description: Cool-season legume with edible sweet pods.
growing_tips: Provide trellis or netting for climbing
Plant in early spring as soon as soil can be worked

description: Warm-season grain grown for fresh eating.
harvest_instructions: Pick when silks are brown and kernels are milky when punctured.
growing_tips: Plant in blocks of at least 4 rows for pollination
Hill soil around base for stability

description: Cool-season crop producing nutritious florets.
harvest_instructions: Cut main head when florets are tight. Side shoots will continue.
growing_tips: Harvest before flowers open

description: Cool-season crop requiring consistent conditions for good heads.
growing_tips: Blanch heads by tying outer leaves over developing curd

description: Cool-season crop producing tight, leafy heads.
growing_tips: Consistent watering prevents splitting

description: Cool-season crop producing small cabbage-like buds along tall stem.

Top plant 4 weeks before harvest to encourage sprout development

description: Warm-season crop with glossy purple, white, or striped fruit.

Harvest when skin is glossy—dull skin means overripe

description: Crisp-stalked vegetable that needs consistent moisture.
growing_tips: Keep soil consistently moist
Blanch stalks by hilling soil or wrapping with cardboard

description: Long-lived perennial that produces spears in spring for 15-20 years.
growing_tips: Don't harvest first 2 years

All-male hybrids produce more spears

description: Heat-loving Southern staple with edible pods.
growing_tips: Soak seeds overnight before planting

description: Quick-growing root vegetable with edible greens.
growing_tips: Both roots and greens are edible

description: Sweet root vegetable that improves in flavor after frost.

description: Mild onion relative prized for its thick white stalk.

description: Unusual brassica with a mild, sweet, swollen stem.
growing_tips: Harvest when bulb is 2-3 inches for best tenderness

description: Peppery salad green that grows quickly in cool weather.

description: Heat-tolerant brassica with large, nutritious leaves.

description: Tart perennial used in pies and preserves. Leaves are toxic.

description: Edible thistle producing large flower buds.

description: Licorice-flavored bulb vegetable with feathery fronds.

description: Woody Mediterranean herb with needle-like leaves.

description: Low-growing herb with tiny aromatic leaves.

description: Lemon-scented herb used in teas and cooking.

description: Daisy-like flowers used for soothing herbal tea.

description: Milder relative of oregano with sweet flavor.

description: Tropical grass with citrus flavor used in Asian cooking.

description: Evergreen tree with aromatic leaves used in cooking.

description: Popular small fruit available as June-bearing or everbearing varieties.
